```{r anova-high-vs-low-type-frequency-print}
    dplyr::arrange(question, population) %>% 
    tidyr::unite(pack.col, question, population, sep = " - ") %>%
    abbreviate_cols() %>% 
    kable.packed("pack.col",
                 caption = "Results from a set of mixed ANOVAs comparing the High Type-Frequency condition to the Low Type-Frequency condition, fitted separately for each sample (online or student) and domain (First-Party Acceptability and Third-Party Acceptability). The models included Valence (between-subjects) and Frequency Condition (within-subjects) as fixed factors, along with their interaction. Significance codes: *** $p \\leq .001$, ** $p \\leq .01$, * $p \\leq .05$, . $p \\leq .10$, ns = not significant.",
                 #col.names = c("", rep(c("N", "M", "SE", "Cohen's d", "p"), 2)),
                 booktabs = TRUE,
                 longtable = TRUE) %>%
    #kableExtra::add_header_above(header = c(" " = 1, "Students" = 5, "Testable" = 5), bold = TRUE) %>%
    kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = c(#"striped",
                                                "scale_down",
                                                "hold_position",
                                                "repeat_header")) %>%
    # kableExtra::row_spec(which(dat.social.type.token.sequential.m.for.table$is_below_chance),
    #                      bold = TRUE, color = "red", background = "gray!20") %>%
    kableExtra::kable_classic_2()

```

As shown in Table \ref{tab:anova-high-vs-low-type-frequency-print}, results from a set of mixed ANOVAs comparing the High Type-Frequency condition to the Low Type-Frequency condition, fitted separately for each sample (online or student) and domain (First-Party Acceptability and Third-Party Acceptability), were similar to those of the CLMMs reported above. The models included Valence (between-subjects) and Frequency Condition (within-subjects) as fixed factors, along with their interaction. There were significant main effects of Frequency Condition and Valence across all domains and samples, and the interaction between them was significant in most conditions.

`r clearpage()`


```{r xxx}
knit_exit()
```


# Unused 
## Power analysis (REMOVE IN PAPER)
We use the SIMR package for power analysis [@Green2016]. 

See https://besjournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/2041-210X.12504 for usage of extend and powerCurve

```{r city-power-prevalence, eval = FALSE}
library (simr)


lmer.social.type.token.sequential.bin.acceptability.other.pwr.numberCond <- 
    powerSim (
        lmer.social.type.token.sequential.bin.acceptability.other.1,
        fcompare(~ myQueriedActionStatus),
        nsim = 10)
# 75.20% power with "bad"" as base-level for valency
# 85% power with "bad"" as base-level for valency

powerSim (
    lmer.social.type.token.sequential.bin.acceptability.other.no.single.1,
    fcompare(~ myQueriedActionStatus),
    nsim = 10)


#lastResult()$errors 
#valency:genderMatch
#questionType:genderMatch


# https://stackoverflow.com/questions/57093394/confirm-succesful-extend-using-simr-package-in-r
# lmer.social.type.tokens.prevalence.bin.city.5.extended <- 
#     extend (
#         lmer.social.type.tokens.prevalence.bin.city.5,
#         #within = "questionType + numberCond + valency + genderMatch + scenarioID",
#         within = "questionType + scenarioID",
#         n = 100)

#getData (social.type.token.females.prevalence.bin.lmer5.extended) %>% 
#    nrow()

# pc <- powerCurve2 (
#     lmer.social.type.tokens.prevalence.bin.city.5.extended,
#     "questionType:valency:genderMatch",
#     #within = "questionType + numberCond + valency + genderMatch + scenarioID",
#     within = "questionType + scenarioID",
#     breaks=seq(50, 100, 10),
#     nsim = 100)

#print(pc)
# 84% power from 70 participants with neutral as base-level for bad

#plot(pc) 


```


`r clearpage()`

## Correlations

```{r correlations-vs-single-calculate}


l.dat.social.type.token.sequential.corr.vs.single <- dat.social.type.token.sequential.m.wide %>% 
    dplyr::group_by(myValence, Machiavellianism, Narcissism, Psychopathy) %>% 
    dplyr::select(matches(fixed ("vs.single_"))) %>% 
    dplyr::select(-ends_with(fixed ("_bin"))) %>%
    dplyr::rename_with(~ .x %>% 
                           str_replace("d\\.(.*)\\.vs.single", "\\1") %>% 
                           str_remove (fixed ("_raw")) %>% 
                           str_replace_all ("_", " - ") %>% 
                           str_replace_all (fixed ("."), " ") %>% 
                           str_wrap(width = 15)) %>% 
    dplyr::group_by(myValence) %>% 
    dplyr::group_split () %>% 
    map (~ list (myValence = levels2 (.$myValence),
                 corrmat = .x %>% 
                     ungroup %>% 
                     dplyr::select(-c(myValence)) %>% 
                     as.matrix %>% 
                     Hmisc::rcorr (.)))



```

```{r extract-correlations-vs-single}
v.predictors <- c("Machiavellianism", "Narcissism", "Psychopathy")

l.dat.social.type.token.sequential.corr.vs.single %>% 
    map (~ .x$corrmat$P %>% 
             as.data.frame() %>% 
             #dplyr::mutate(across (everything(), ~ .x <= .05)) %>% 
             tibble::rownames_to_column ("var") %>% 
             dplyr::select(-v.predictors) %>% 
             dplyr::rowwise() %>% 
             dplyr::mutate(keep.row = any (dplyr::c_across(-c("var")) <= .05, na.rm= TRUE)) %>% 
             dplyr::filter(keep.row) %>% 
             dplyr::select (-keep.row) %>% 
             dplyr::filter(var %in% v.predictors) %>% 
             tibble::column_to_rownames("var") %>% 
             tdf %>% 
             tibble::rownames_to_column("var") %>% 
             dplyr::rowwise() %>% 
             dplyr::mutate(keep.row = any (dplyr::c_across(-c("var")) <= .05, na.rm= TRUE)) %>% 
             dplyr::filter(keep.row) %>% 
             dplyr::select (-keep.row) %>% 
             dplyr::mutate(myValence = .x$myValence, .before = 1)) %>% 
    dplyr::bind_rows() %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(var = str_replace_all (var, fixed ("\n"), " ")) %>%
    kable.packed (
        "myValence",
        caption = "P values of significant correlations between dark triad predictors and difference scores comparing ratings to the equivalent ratings in the single occurrence condition,",
        escape = TRUE,
       booktabs = TRUE) %>%
kableExtra::kable_classic()
    

```

```{r correlations-high-vs-low-calculate}


l.dat.social.type.token.sequential.corr.high.vs.low <- dat.social.type.token.sequential.m.wide %>% 
    dplyr::group_by(myValence, Machiavellianism, Narcissism, Psychopathy) %>% 
    dplyr::select(starts_with(fixed ("d.highTypeFreq.vs.lowTypeFreq_"))) %>% 
    dplyr::select(-ends_with(fixed ("_bin"))) %>%
    dplyr::rename_with(~ .x %>% 
                           str_remove(fixed ("d.highTypeFreq.vs.lowTypeFreq_")) %>% 
                           str_remove (fixed ("_raw")) %>% 
                           str_replace_all ("_", " - ") %>% 
                           str_replace_all (fixed ("."), " ") %>% 
                           str_wrap(width = 15)) %>% 
    dplyr::group_by(myValence) %>% 
    dplyr::group_split () %>% 
    map (~ list (myValence = levels2 (.$myValence),
                 corrmat = .x %>% 
                     ungroup %>% 
                     dplyr::select(-c(myValence)) %>% 
                     as.matrix %>% 
                     Hmisc::rcorr (.)))



```

```{r extract-correlations-high-vs-low}

l.dat.social.type.token.sequential.corr.high.vs.low %>% 
    map (~ .x$corrmat$P %>% 
             as.data.frame() %>% 
             #dplyr::mutate(across (everything(), ~ .x <= .05)) %>% 
             tibble::rownames_to_column ("var") %>% 
             dplyr::select(-v.predictors) %>% 
             dplyr::rowwise() %>% 
             dplyr::mutate(keep.row = any (dplyr::c_across(-c("var")) <= .05, na.rm= TRUE)) %>% 
             dplyr::filter(keep.row) %>% 
             dplyr::select (-keep.row) %>% 
             dplyr::filter(var %in% v.predictors) %>% 
             tibble::column_to_rownames("var") %>% 
             tdf %>% 
             tibble::rownames_to_column("var") %>% 
             dplyr::rowwise() %>% 
             dplyr::mutate(keep.row = any (dplyr::c_across(-c("var")) <= .05, na.rm= TRUE)) %>% 
             dplyr::filter(keep.row) %>% 
             dplyr::select (-keep.row) %>% 
             dplyr::mutate(myValence = .x$myValence, .before = 1)) %>% 
    dplyr::bind_rows() %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(var = str_replace_all (var, fixed ("\n"), " ")) %>%
    kable.packed (
        "myValence",
        caption = "P values of significant correlations between dark triad predictors and difference scores comparing comparing the high and the low type-frequency conditions.",
        escape = TRUE,
       booktabs = TRUE) %>%
kableExtra::kable_classic()


```

```{r correlations-vs-single-plot, fig.cap = "Correlation for difference scores comparing actions against single occurrence actions.", fig.height=17}


par(mfrow=c(3, 1),  
    mar = c(1, 1, 1, 1))
    #xpd = TRUE)

l.dat.social.type.token.sequential.corr.vs.single %>% 
    purrr::walk(~ corrplot::corrplot(.x$corrmat$r,
                       is.corr = TRUE,
                       type = "lower",
                       diag = FALSE,
                       tl.col = "blue",
                       tl.cex = 1.5,
                       cl.cex = .5,
                       #bg = "gray",
                       tl.srt = 75,
                       title = str_c ("\n\n\n\n\n", .x$myValence),
                       addCoef.col = "black",
                       addgrid.col = "black",
                       #order = "hclust",
                       p.mat = .x$corrmat$P,
                       insig = "label_sig",
                       sig.level = .05,
                       mar = c(1, 1, 1, 1)
                       ))






```

```{r correlations-high-vs-low-plot, fig.cap = "Correlation for difference scores comparing high vs. low type-frequency actions.", fig.height=17}


par(mfrow=c(3, 1),  
    mar = c(1, 1, 1, 1))
    #xpd = TRUE)

l.dat.social.type.token.sequential.corr.high.vs.low %>% 
    purrr::walk(~ corrplot::corrplot(.x$corrmat$r,
                       is.corr = TRUE,
                       type = "lower",
                       diag = FALSE,
                       tl.col = "blue",
                       tl.cex = 1.5,
                       cl.cex = .5,
                       #bg = "gray",
                       tl.srt = 75,
                       title = str_c ("\n\n\n\n\n", .x$myValence),
                       addCoef.col = "black",
                       addgrid.col = "black",
                       #order = "hclust",
                       p.mat = .x$corrmat$P,
                       insig = "label_sig",
                       sig.level = .05,
                       mar = c(1, 1, 1, 1)
                       ))






```
